frame: Refugee/humanitarian path sets employment ramp, permanence horizon, and mortality multiplier for lifetime NPV. CDC 2021 Hispanic e₀ and Clemens LMA synthesis bound forward-stock years; Dustmann et al. document EU employment integration lag.

parameter_claims.population: refugees vs natives, EU15+NOR+CHE, 2008 EU LFS
parameter_claims.notes: 16.1pp unconditional gap vs 7.2pp non-EU15 economic migrants. Unnamed: EU 2008 refugee integration generalizes to U.S. humanitarian entrants.

parameter_claims.population: refugees 0–3 years since arrival vs natives
parameter_claims.notes: 50pp less likely employed — legal work bans during asylum processing. Critical early-year tax floor = near zero for lifetime NPV front-end.
Gap halves by 7–10 years; statistically insignificant at 15–19 years; approaches zero at 25 years. Cross-section only — cohort caveat.

parameter_claims.population: refugees from North Africa/Middle East vs natives, conditional on age/sex/education
parameter_claims.notes: Largest origin-area gap; exceeds economic migrants from same region. Maps to Syrian/Afghan-heavy 2015 EU inflow.
Nearly double 1992 peak (850k). Syria 16.9%, Afghanistan 10.2%. Germany 914k applications; Sweden 450.8 per 10k population.
~10% led to recognition (GCR or subsidiary). Unnamed: subsidiary protection duration mapped to U.S. TPS/parole fiscal horizon.

parameter_claims.population: Hispanic origin, both sexes, U.S. 2021 period life table
parameter_claims.notes: −0.1yr from 77.9 (2020). +1.4yr over total population e₀=76.4. Unnamed: Hispanic table applied to refugee/Latin American immigrant cells regardless of nativity/generation.
Male 50.8, female 55.2. Drives benefit-cost horizon for young humanitarian entrants. Medicare modeling uses statistical old-age imputation for Hispanic (no direct Medicare data).
Retirement-phase weight for SS/Medicare NPV. Hispanic e₆₅ exceeds Black (17.6) below White NH — mortality heterogeneity affects PAYG phase.
Net positive fiscal contributor after 8 years. Marbach: sooner work access → faster positive path. Unnamed: EU employment lag (50pp yr-3) compatible with U.S. 8yr breakeven.

generators.name: Refugee employment ramp calibrator
generators.prompt: For humanitarian/refugee lifetime cells, impose employment ramp from Dustmann et al.: −50pp year 0–3, −16pp unconditional steady-state gap, convergence ~15–25 years. Do not use economic-migrant employment rates in arrival year. Pair with Clemens 8-year U.S. fiscal breakeven as cross-check.
generators.retrodiction: Would have blocked flat employment assumption for parole/asylum entrants before forward-stock NPV.
generators.negative_space: Refugees enter at native-average employment and earnings in year 1.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: EU 2008 LFS employment gaps apply to U.S. refugee cohorts
Employment gap = earnings gap for federal microsim
generators.theory_tested: Refugee employment integration follows steep ramp with 15–25 year convergence

generators.name: Mortality forward-stock multiplier
generators.prompt: When projecting SS/Medicare NPV for Hispanic/refugee cells, require explicit life-table anchor: CDC 2021 Hispanic e₀=77.8, e₂₅=53.8, e₆₅=19.3 vs total-pop 76.4. Flag use of White NH or generic SSA tables without Hispanic adjustment. Note Medicare imputation uncertainty for Hispanic old-age mortality.
generators.retrodiction: Would have surfaced +1.4yr Hispanic e₀ vs total before applying uniform 75-year NAS horizon to Latin American humanitarian entrants.
generators.negative_space: Single life table for all immigrant origins in lifetime model.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Hispanic-origin life table represents foreign-born refugee mortality experience
Period 2021 life table stable for cohort projections
generators.theory_tested: Hispanic life expectancy extends benefit-cost horizon ~1–2 years vs total population

generators.name: Permanence vs subsidiary protection fork
generators.prompt: Dustmann: full GCR asylum → permanent settlement vs subsidiary/temporary protection → return incentive → lower host-country human-capital investment. For U.S. parole/TPS/asylum pending, branch lifetime NPV into {permanent, temporary, rejected} with different education/employment investment paths. EU ~10% recognition rate is lower-bound permanence probability.
generators.retrodiction: Would have split Pew 40% temp-protection subshare into permanence branches before scalar unauthorized NPV.
generators.negative_space: All humanitarian entrants eventually permanent with identical integration path.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Subsidiary protection equals permanent settlement for NPV
Recognition rate stable across origin groups
generators.theory_tested: Temporary protection truncates human-capital investment and lowers long-run fiscal contribution

generators.name: Formal LMA breakeven clock
generators.prompt: Clemens WP496: formal labor market access is lever once refugees already present — avg U.S. refugee net fiscal positive at 8 years; work-access timing shifts breakeven (Marbach). For each humanitarian cell, declare LMA state {banned yr0-3, informal, formal} and map to Evans-Fitzgerald clock.
generators.retrodiction: Would have paired Clemens 8-year breakeven with Dustmann 50pp yr-3 employment ban before assuming humanitarian entrants are permanent fiscal negatives.
generators.negative_space: Work authorization irrelevant to refugee fiscal path.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: U.S. 8-year breakeven applies to 2021–2023 parole surge without adjustment
Formal LMA equals immediate employment at native rates
generators.theory_tested: Granting formal LMA accelerates refugee net fiscal contribution inside decade horizon

generators.name: Origin-area employment penalty mapper
generators.prompt: Dustmann Figure 9: MENA refugees −32.5pp conditional employment vs natives; other Africa/Asia also elevated. When modeling Syrian/Afghan/Venezuelan humanitarian cells, do not use Mexico <HS employment defaults — apply origin-area penalty decay schedule from EU evidence.
generators.retrodiction: Would have differentiated MENA humanitarian employment penalty from Mexico economic-migrant path in lifetime ledger.
generators.negative_space: All humanitarian entrants share one employment gap regardless of origin.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: EU MENA refugee gaps transfer to U.S. MENA humanitarian entrants
Conditional gap persistence equals unconditional fiscal effect
generators.theory_tested: MENA-origin refugees face larger employment penalty than European humanitarian migrants

theories_tested.theory: Refugee lifetime fiscal sign flips positive after ~8–15 year employment integration ramp
theories_tested.prediction: Humanitarian cell NPV negative in years 0–7 (50pp employment gap, near-zero tax), crosses zero between years 8–15 (Clemens/Evans-Fitzgerald), positive thereafter if permanence assumed
theories_tested.duckdb_test: Scenario compose: apply dustmann_employment_ramp {0.5, 0.16, 0} gap decay to acs humanitarian proxy cell; discount at SSA 3%; compare cumulative federal balance to year-8 and year-15 thresholds
theories_tested.falsifier: Cell remains net negative at year 15 under plausible earnings growth and no exit

theories_tested.theory: Hispanic life-table e₀ (+1.4yr over total) lengthens PAYG benefit phase for Latin American humanitarian entrants
theories_tested.prediction: Using Hispanic e₂₅=53.8 vs total 52.5 increases SS/Medicare NPV cost by ~2–3% relative to total-race table for same earnings path
theories_tested.duckdb_test: Pipe identical earnings stream through cdc_hispanic vs cdc_total survivorship to age 85; diff SS/Medicare liability in microsim
theories_tested.falsifier: Mortality adjustment changes lifetime NPV <0.5% — negligible for policy conclusions

theories_tested.theory: Subsidiary/temporary protection lowers long-run fiscal contribution via truncated human-capital investment
theories_tested.prediction: Branch with 60% temporary / 40% permanent (Pew temp-protection scale) yields lower year-25 NPV than 100% permanent despite lower year-0 costs
theories_tested.duckdb_test: Two-branch Monte Carlo: permanent branch gets Clemens formal-LMA uplift after year 3; temporary branch exits or low employment after year 10; compare expected NPV
theories_tested.falsifier: Temporary branch expected NPV ≥ permanent branch under reasonable recognition probabilities
